summary: A solid matrix mix of the Dylan‑Dead collaboration, highlighted by an inspired Rainy Day Women performance.
review: Both reviewers praise this July 19 1987 show where the Grateful Dead back Bob Dylan, noting a strong musical chemistry and a particularly memorable Rainy Day Women. The SbdAud (matrix) recording is described as "pretty nice" though the mix could have featured more Phil on bass and clearer Dylan vocals. Overall it’s an enjoyable set with good sound quality and a fun blend of artists.

ai_rating.rationale: High star ratings (4‑5) and enthusiastic comments about the performance outweigh only minor recording mix critiques, leading to a strong combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Recording is generally clear and pleasant; reviewers note it could use more bass presence and slightly better vocal balance but no major flaws.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A unique Dylan‑Dead set where the Dead serve as backing band, creating a cohesive blend of folk‑rock with classic Dead improvisation.

band_member_comments.Phil: Reviewers wished for more Phil Lesh on bass in the mix.
